programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: A group of high school outcasts are on the run through the Southeastern United States after their leader commits a savage double homicide; Cops finally catch the thrill-seeking teens, only to find out they're a makeshift vampire cult.

programme.title: It Takes a Killer
programme.desc: When a wife and mother overdoses in a small Texas town, almost no one suspects foul play; the family forces investigators to dig deeper, only to find out she was murdered by the last person anyone would have expected.

programme.desc: A murder victim's final moments are caught on tape; the crime scene looks like a clear robbery-murder; text evidence at first points to a jilted lover, but police identify a second suspect with a motive.

programme.title: Forensic Files II
programme.desc: A wounded woman calls 911; her husband, who wants a divorce, gets a hard look from police, but he has an airtight alibi during the murder; forensic science of ballistics draws a direct connection.

programme.title: Forensic Files II

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Detectives are suspicious after a woman is killed in a hunting accident, but it takes more than twenty years before a trace evidence analyst is able to match fibers on a soiled blanket to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When hunters find a skull in a Texas canyon, police find bits of clothing, a woman's shoe, some small bones and a strand of hair; once they discover her identity, it was time to find the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Suicide notes and a pistol in hand lead police to suspect that a mother of two young children appeared to have killed herself, but when investigators learned of a house fire she was in three years earlier, they reinvestigated.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: Investigating the unsolved 2015 murder of John Palmer, who was nicknamed Goldfinger after his involvement in melting down gold from the 1983 Brink's-Mat robbery. Palmer was found dead in his Essex garden and while police originally believed he died of natural causes, they eventually confirmed the case was being treated as a professional hit.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: The search for John Palmer's killer leads to Tenerife, where he built a timeshare empire. Undercover stings and Russian mob ties expose the scale of his power.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: A professional hitman is suspected in John Palmer's murder, with evidence pointing to a cartel and an Eastern European enforcer.

programme.title: Ren Faire
programme.desc: George Coulam goes on an unsuccessful date, while Jeff Baldwin struggles to thwart Louie Migliaccio's bid for control of the Texas Renaissance Festival and former elephant trainer Darla Smith enters the fray with an agenda of her own.

programme.title: Ren Faire

programme.title: Man on Wire
programme.desc: Oscar-winning documentary about French street performer Philippe Petit. In 1974 he undertook a daring 45-minute tightrope walk and acrobatic routine on a wire stretched between the two towers of the World Trade Centre. Featuring re-creations of his earlier stunts at Paris's Notre Dame cathedral and Sydney Harbour Bridge.

programme.title: Be Water
